seoTitle: Sorrento vs South Bodallin — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Sorrento and South Bodallin. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Sorrento vs South Bodallin: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sorrento and South Bodallin.
heading: Sorrento vs South Bodallin — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sorrento, Western Australia and South Bodallin, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Bodallin has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Sorrento (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sorrento or South Bodallin safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sorrento has a flood risk score of 46/100, while South Bodallin scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sorrento scores 63/100 for bushfire risk. South Bodallin scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sorrento or South Bodallin?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Bodallin has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.93% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
